---
title: "conjunction"
generated: true
description: "Minimum-statistic conjunction across `k` contrasts."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons`](../nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:conjunction -->
```python
conjunction(stats: Float[Array, 'k *spatial']) -> Float[Array, '*spatial']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:conjunction -->
Minimum-statistic conjunction across `k` contrasts.

Returns the per-element minimum statistic over the `k` contrasts, the valid
statistic for the conjunction null *"at least one of the `k` effects is
null"*: an element survives a threshold only where every contrast clears it,
which is exactly where the minimum clears it. This is the correct conjunction;
the common "all-effects-null" intersection of separately-thresholded maps
tests a different (global) null and does not control the conjunction error.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:conjunction -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `stats` | `Float` | `k` × `*spatial` | — | The `k` per-contrast statistic maps stacked on the leading axis, each of shape `*spatial`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `*spatial`. The per-element minimum statistic over the `k` contrasts.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:conjunction -->
### References

Nichols, T., Brett, M., Andersson, J., Wager, T., & Poline, J.-B. (2005).
Valid conjunction inference with the minimum statistic. NeuroImage, 25(3),
653-660. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2004.12.005
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:conjunction -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
